summary: The Goshen Township Board of Zoning Appeals held public hearings on three remanded cases involving property at 6823 Goshen Road: two administrative appeals (fence violation and stop work order for an unpermitted automotive repair shop) and a use variance request. After hearing testimony from staff, the applicant Jeff Kelly, former zoning director Bob Seifert, supporters, and opponents, the Board denied all three applications. The meeting also included oath of office ceremonies for board members and election of officers for the new year.
decisions: John Langenheim elected as Vice Chairperson
Tom Currie nominated and elected as Secretary
BZA-2022-011 (fence administrative appeal) denied - administrative decision upheld
BZA-2022-012 (stop work order administrative appeal) denied - administrative decision upheld
BZA-2022-013 (use variance for automotive repair shop) denied per staff recommendation
action_items: Staff to report back to the Court of Common Pleas judge after Board determinations on all three cases
Further court proceedings to follow Board decisions
key_amounts: $3,300 per year in tax revenue from the subject parcel cited by applicant
4,800 square foot pole barn garage at issue
100-foot minimum setback required from adjacent residential lot vs. 28 feet actual distance
6-foot privacy fence constructed where 4-foot maximum with 50% transparency is required
notable_topics: Administrative appeal of fence violation (height, transparency, and orientation violations)
Administrative appeal of stop work order for unpermitted automotive repair shop in R-3 residential zone
Use variance request to operate automotive repair heavy in R-3 residential district
Dispute over original building permit and whether it authorized commercial use vs. agricultural barn
Oath of office for Board members and election of 2024 officers
Court remand of previously heard cases from 2022
Setback requirements for commercial buildings near residential lots
Testimony from former zoning director Bob Seifert contradicting applicant's claims
attendees: John Langenheim (Board member, elected Vice Chair)
Tom Currie (Board member, elected Secretary - absent)
Mr. Curry (Board member - absent at roll call)
Mr. Holmes (Board member - absent at roll call)
Francis 'Buddy' Kelly (6825 Goshen Road, applicant supporter)
Taylor Trout (Applicant's attorney, referenced in letter)
notes: Cases were originally heard in 2022 and remanded back to the BZA by Clermont County Court of Common Pleas in May 2023 for new hearings. The applicant Jeff Kelly did not present testimony or participate in the stop work order (BZA-2022-012) or use variance (BZA-2022-013) hearings, appearing to have left or declined to participate. Former zoning director Bob Seifert testified that the original permit was for an agricultural barn and denied telling Kelly he could operate a business. An opponent presented detailed legal arguments citing multiple Ohio court cases regarding setback variances and legislative vs. quasi-judicial functions. The Board conducted a deliberative session in private before voting, which was noted as permitted for quasi-judicial boards.
title: BZA 1-22-24
